title: The EBA responds to the European Commission’s consultation on EU banking sector competitiveness
clean_title: The EBA responds to the European Commission’s consultation on EU banking sector competitiveness
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) has today published its response to the European Commission’s consultation on strengthening the competitiveness of the EU banking sector. In its response, the EBA underlines the importance of completing the Single Market for financial services, as a key driver of banking competitiveness. Drawing on its robust analytical work, the EBA provides evidence-based insights on banking competitiveness, the Single Market and the Banking Union, as well as on the complexity and effectiveness of the regulatory framework.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: commission competitiveness consultation eba european responds sector
normalized_title: the eba responds to the european commission s consultation on eu banking sector competitiveness

title: Christine Lagarde: IMFC Statement
summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) is the central bank of the European Union countries which have adopted the euro. Our main task is to maintain price stability in the euro area and so preserve the purchasing power of the single currency.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european central bank) ; information très récente.
impacts: Impact métier modéré mais utile pour maintenir une veille sectorielle structurée.
